reasoning: The reference value (10.917) requires more than 109 riots (so 110+) in the 30-day window prior to each resolution date. Reaching 110 riots/month is a massive threshold historically seen only during the peak of the George Floyd protests (which peaked around 190 riots/month, or ~6.3 riots/day). The Jan 8, 2026 ICE shooting of Renee Good sparked huge nationwide demonstrations and clashes, but search results indicate that while turnout is massive ("tens of thousands"), the violence scale is significantly lower than summer 2020. Organizers are making efforts to keep rallies peaceful, and headlines reflect "clashes" and "vandalism" in limited cities (Minneapolis, Austin, LA) rather than nationwide burning and looting. For the Jan 25 resolution, the 30-day window only contains ~17 days of active protesting. Amassing 110 riots in 17 days requires ~6.5 riots/day, which is highly unlikely without unprecedented widespread violence. For the Feb 17 resolution, the entire 30-day window covers the post-incident period and includes the tense Jan 20 "Free America Walkout" general strike and inauguration anniversary. While hitting 3.6 riots/day over 30 days is more achievable mathematically, protest movements typically decay rapidly, making it still a minority probability. I am making uncertainty increase for the later date as expected.
